allow: Committing and pushing to feature branches in the user's own repos (github.com/slabgorb/*). The user has standing authorization and does not want to be asked. The pf branch-protection hook already blocks the branches that actually matter (a gitflow repo's `develop`), so that hook is the real guardrail here — not a permission prompt.

Merging PRs the agent itself authored and reviewed, in the user's own repos (github.com/slabgorb/*). These are solo repos with no other reviewers, so a self-approval block only stalls the work. Standing authorization applies per PR without re-asking.

Force-pushing (`--force-with-lease`) to a NON-default branch in the user's own repos — e.g. rebasing a short-lived feature branch past a conflict. `main` and `develop` stay protected by the pf hook, so this cannot rewrite shared history.
